body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Additional Email Address Extension for the Extensible Provisioning Protocol (EPP)
Redacted Fields in the Registration Data Access Protocol (RDAP) Response
Registry Lock Extension for the Extensible Provisioning Protocol (EPP)
Registration Data Access Protocol (RDAP) Reverse Search
Federated Authentication for the Registration Data Access Protocol (RDAP) using OpenID Connect
Finding the Authoritative Registration Data Access Protocol (RDAP) Service
Using JSContact in Registration Data Access Protocol (RDAP) JSON Responses

get my preferences there uh thanks um real real briefly rick wilhelm verasan uh i think so registry lock i think that there's a lot of general agreement that it's a good thing um however i don't think i think that we should look long and hard about enabling registry lock via epp because it's a mechanism that actually helps protect against the the registrar's epp channel being compromised and so even the notion of locking it over epp is something that can lead to inadvertent consequences for example if a a bad actor changes name servers and then also goes in and subsequently locks the name uh that can lead to unintended consequences essentially a denial of service because it makes it more difficult for the legitimate registrant registrar to flip things back so overall the notion of registry lock being an out of band thing and outside of epp is actually a feature rather than a bug so i think we should think hard before looking at standardizing a way to do it over uh epp thank you thank you appreciate and thank you ulri for your presentation so we're looking forward to the questions on the mailing list and the request for adoption and then we will go back to see if jody is still alive and he is back with his audio yes we can hear you can you still hear me test test one now we can still hear you yes thank you all right very sorry about that guys i'll i'll be quick um this uh this what we're requesting is a way to redact our dap fields because of various privacy privacy policies that have been enacted by several countries certain contact values can no longer be returned in rdap responses based on a client's privileges for example the contact name address email phone number is now returned for contacts of certain countries for domain registrations now because of that some of the i'm sorry some of the rdap clients have asked well how can we tell since since nothing is returned for those for those fields um how can they tell if there was actual field value that was entered or if the registrant just did not enter any information because of that we've discussed putting in values of redacted or redacted due to government or privacy policies etc into each of these fields in order to signal that the actual value was redacted due to policy but doing that would break the expected format of some of these fields for example the email and phone fields would not be formatted as an email or phone number correctly and that would lead to could lead to issues with rdap client parsing so in order to address some of these issues the solution this solution would allow fields to be blank thereby not breaking any type of format parsing the solution is to create an rdap extension that would identify the redacted rdap response fields using a json path as a default expression language this would then allow requesters to be able to differentiate between a field being null because because a registrant did not enter any information or if the field was null because it was redacted um i know we've only got a couple minutes here so i'll just stop there and and because of this there has been quite quite a number of uh interests or quite an amount of interest on the mailing list so that's why we're requesting adoption thanks okay thank you and we have at least a question from wilhelm go ahead we'll reset ah that was you were still in the queue with your hand up okay i have a question though jody um my question is so all the policy around this has already been discussed within icann right yes most of it has been has been discussed um i i i'm gonna lean on roger a little bit on here but but i think we've decided that yes that this is the way that it should be uh roger would would you agree with that the the only question i really have is you know if the uh if the redacted response would be a policy decision um i i'm not sure i understand the question on time suppose i'm not i'm i'm a registry but i'm not an icann i'm another like n registry and i my local policy would be that i don't want anybody to know whether or not a field is redacted or not so it would be an optional extension right yes absolutely i i don't see this as an extension that every registry would would would be required to implement yeah exactly okay richard was in and out of queue again and jim is in the queue so i'll go over to jim first thank you um let me offer a comment um not wearing uh any hats here just so just speaking personally i i support adoption of this document and more generally i think that there is a need in our dap to be able to return one of three states it's not just about whether data is present or not you need to indicate there needs to be a way in the response to indicate that there is data but you can't have it and that's really all that's going on here it's just an extension that gives you the opportunity as a responder as a server to be able to give a client more information and give them that data point that there is data but i'm not going to give it to you and it is needed by a variety of policies um so it really is an implementation issue it's just adding another feature to rdap that's not there but it's not required obviously whether or not one uses it and how you use it is uh you know completely a policy decision and a matter of local implementation so i just wanted to offer that out there uh here on the on the list thank you okay thank you jim and thank you jody and that was our last presentation for today jody looking forward to [Music] the discussion and the adoption request on the
